ops.title: Fleet sweeps scope to ALL active yasyf repos, not just the named prefix — user corrected a cc-*-only CI sweep
ops.body:
When Yasyf asks for a fleet-wide operation ("our repos", "our cc-* repos have failing CI"), the named prefix is an example, not a boundary — sweep every active repo (`gh repo list yasyf --no-archived --source`, pushed within ~90 days; ~44 repos as of 2026-07-08, including private ones like ego, stream-trade, yasyf.com, rescue-chai).

**Why:** a cc-*-scoped CI sweep (2026-07-08) missed failing CI on captain-hook, fusekit, yclaw, dailies, summ, homebrew-do/summ, bitfieldable, and synonym-to-clicky; the user asked "why didn't you check all our relevant repos?"

**How to apply:** start any "fix the fleet" task with the full active-repo enumeration and a latest-run-per-workflow sweep, then triage relevance (active vs parked vs archive-candidates) explicitly in the report rather than by silently narrowing scope. Related: [[model-routing-convention]].
